senses.definition: A rare disease in which fat deposits build up in the heart muscle.
examples: The doctor mentioned a rare condition involving fat in the heart muscle.
Tests suggested fat-like changes in the heart tissue.

senses.definition: Softening (damage) of the heart muscle, often due to poor blood supply.
examples: The term cardiomyomalacia describes damage to the heart muscle.
Her cardiomyomalacia was linked to reduced blood flow.

senses.definition: Diseases of the heart muscle that make it harder for the heart to pump blood.
examples: Cardiomyopathies can cause shortness of breath and tiredness.
The patient was diagnosed with a cardiomyopathy after scans.

senses.definition: A disease of the heart muscle that affects how well the heart pumps blood.
examples: Cardiomyopathy can make the heart muscle weak or stiff.
He was treated for cardiomyopathy after having dizziness and swelling.

senses.definition: A general medical category covering different problems with the myocardium (heart muscle).
examples: Doctors may group several conditions under cardiomyopathy.
Her cardiomyopathy type was identified as dilated.

senses.definition: A term used in medical/academic writing (rare or uncommon) that refers to a growth or lesion in the heart region.
examples: The report listed cardioncus as a possible term for the finding.
Specialist review was needed to confirm what cardioncus meant.

senses.definition: Death of heart tissue (necrosis), often caused by lack of blood and oxygen.
examples: Cardionecrosis can occur after a severe blockage in a coronary artery.
Imaging showed areas consistent with cardionecrosis.

senses.definition: Related to both the heart (cardio-) and the kidneys (nephric).
examples: The cardi-on-ephric system involves the heart and the kidneys.
Her doctor discussed a cardionephric connection between heart and kidney function.

senses.definition: Related to both the heart (cardio-) and the nervous system (neural).
examples: The cardi-on-eural pathway affects how the heart and nerves work together.
Researchers studied cardioneural signaling in lab experiments.

senses.definition: An older/rare term sometimes used for anxiety-related heart symptoms without clear heart damage.
examples: In the past, doctors used cardioneurosis to describe nervous-system-related heart complaints.
Her symptoms were discussed as a possible cardioneurosis in older records.

senses.definition: A rare or unclear medical term that appears to refer to a disease/condition in the heart region.
examples: The term cardionosus was used in the text, but the exact meaning was not clear.
A specialist clarified that cardionosus referred to a heart-related condition.

senses.definition: A rare medical term meaning abnormal growth or formation related to the heart.
examples: The report used cardioparplasis as a possible label for abnormal growth.
Further tests were needed to understand the meaning of cardioparplasis.

senses.definition: A person who has a heart disease.
examples: He is a cardiopath and needs regular check-ups.
Many cardiopaths manage their condition with medication and lifestyle changes.

senses.definition: Relating to or caused by a heart disease.
examples: The cardiopathic condition required specialist treatment.
His symptoms were described as cardiopathic in the early medical notes.

senses.definition: A medical term for heart diseases or disorders affecting the heart.
examples: Cardiopathies can include problems with the heart muscle and heart function.
The doctor used cardiopathies to describe a group of heart disorders.

senses.definition: A disease or disorder of the heart.
examples: Cardiopathy is a general word for heart disease.
She was diagnosed with cardiopathy after an examination.

senses.definition: Inflammation affecting both the heart muscle (myocardium) and the lining around the heart (pericardium).
examples: Cardiopericarditis can cause chest pain and fever.
Her doctors diagnosed cardiopericarditis after tests showed inflammation in both areas.

senses.definition: A person who has a fear of the heart or fear connected to heart disease.
examples: A cardiophobe may worry intensely about heart symptoms.
Therapy can help a cardiophobe feel safer about health concerns.

senses.definition: A strong fear or anxiety about the heart or heart disease.
examples: Cardiophobia can cause frequent checking of symptoms.
She developed cardiophobia after learning about heart problems in someone close to her.

senses.definition: A rare or unclear term that appears to refer to a heart-related mind/behavior problem (meaning not standard in common contemporary English).
examples: The text used cardiophrenia, but the meaning was not fully clear.
The reader needed context to understand cardiophrenia in that document.

senses.definition: Surgery to repair or improve the heart.
examples: Cardioplasty is performed to fix certain heart problems.
She had a cardioplasty after doctors found a structural defect.

senses.definition: A medical technique where a special solution is used to temporarily stop the heart during surgery.
examples: During the operation, doctors used cardioplegia to protect the heart.
Cardioplegia helps surgeons work while the heart is temporarily stopped.

senses.definition: Different instances or types of cardioplegia used during heart surgery.
examples: The report mentions cardioplegias used during the procedures.
Doctors selected from several cardioplegias based on the patient’s needs.

senses.definition: Related to both the heart and air pressure or breathing-related devices.
examples: The device was described as cardiopneumatic in the technical manual.
Cardiopneumatic equipment can be used in certain medical settings.

senses.definition: A medical instrument that records heart and breathing-related signals together.
examples: A cardiopneumograph can help doctors monitor changes over time.
The cardiopneumograph produced traces showing heart and lung activity.

senses.definition: A rare medical term referring to a downward displacement (drooping) related to the heart.
examples: Cardioptosis was mentioned as a possible structural issue.
More tests were needed to confirm cardioptosis.
